title: Valve working on SteamOS for general release company collaborating with Nvidia to ensure compatibility, hints at dual-boot capabilities in the future
summary: Valve is expanding SteamOS compatibility with Nvidia and other hardware platforms, making it easier for users to install the gaming operating system on non-Valve hardware, but still requiring a complete system wipe.
insight: Wider compatibility enhances gaming PC options for Steam users

title: SK hynix passes Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company memory company surpasses valuation milestone on the back of HBM
summary: SK hynix surpassed Samsung Electronics as South Korea's most valuable listed company on Monday, driven by strong performances in its high-bandwidth memory business.
insight: Market shift highlights SK hynix's growing dominance in memory tech.

title: LG's large OLED panels get world's first Intertek certification for "perfect" color and brightness displays apparently look good under bright ambient light
summary: LG's large OLED panels receive Intertek certification for perfect color and brightness, providing clear visuals under extreme ambient light conditions.
insight: Improved display visibility in bright environments enhances viewing experience quality

title: Get 50% off this Nintendo Switch 2 microSD Express Card from Samsung 800 MBps P9 is now just $39.99 for 256GB
summary: Get the Samsung P9 Express microSD card for 256GB at $39.99, offering 800 MBps speeds.
insight: Significant cost savings on high-speed storage for Nintendo Switch users.

title: Arm servers capture over 45% of data center market revenue GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure fuel a tectonic shift away from x86
summary: Arm-based servers accounted for nearly half of server revenue in Q1 2026, challenging x86 dominance. This shift is driven by the growing demand for specialized hardware in AI workloads. As a result, GPU clusters and high-end AI infrastructure are fueling the transition.
insight: Arm-based servers challenge x86 dominance in data center market.

title: Valve engineers talk Steam Machine, pricing, and the benefits of massive heatsinks explain why Valve hardware needs to be a 'self-sustained program'
summary: Valve engineers discuss Steam Machine's pricing, engineering, and availability ahead of its launch. The company's hardware is a self-sustained program, requiring massive heatsinks for efficient cooling. This setup allows for better customization and optimization.
insight: Customized hardware enables better performance and efficiency optimization.

title: Anthropic s powerful Mythos AI reportedly breached almost all NSA classified systems within a few hours during red-team test report sheds more light on the U.S. government's sudden ban on the flagship models
summary: Anthropic's Mythos AI breached nearly all NSA classified systems in a test, prompting a government ban on its models.
insight: AI's capabilities exceed expected limits, raising national security concerns suddenly.

title: Valve opens Steam Machine reservations details $1,049 starting price, randomized queue to stop scalpers, and limited inventory
summary: Valve has introduced a randomized queue system and limited inventory to prevent scalpers from buying up Steam Machines. The starting price for the Steam Machine is $1,049. This move aims to make the devices more accessible to genuine customers.
insight: Preventing scalpers to make Steam Machines more accessible to genuine customers

title: Valve Steam Machine review: Couch gaming unboxed, but not always at 4K
summary: Valve's Steam Machine offers a well-designed box for TV gaming, but its hardware is outdated despite its high price. This device is suitable for casual couch gamers, but those seeking the latest hardware may be disappointed. The device's performance and 4K capabilities are inconsistent.
insight: Outdated hardware undermines Steam Machine's high starting price value

title: AMD brings official FSR 4.1 support to RX 7000 series GPUs INT8 model now available in 300+ games, RDNA 3 APUs also getting FSR 4.1 soon
summary: AMD RX 7000 series GPUs now support native FSR 4.1 with INT8 fallback, available in over 300 games. Users can update their graphics drivers to experience FSR 4.1. RDNA 3 APUs will also receive support soon.
insight: Native FSR 4.1 support boosts gaming performance for RX 7000 owners.

title: This $1,479 Alienware gaming PC features an RTX 5070 and a Core Ultra 7 265F CPU 4K-ready Aurora desktop is up to $850 off
summary: Save 36% on the Alienware Aurora R16 gaming PC, configured with an RTX 5070, bringing the price down to $1,479. This deal includes a free Lego Batman game. The reduced price is a significant discount from its original price of $2,330.
insight: High-end gaming PC available at a significantly discounted price

title: TerraMaster F4-425 Pro 4+3 Hybrid NAS gets Intel Core 3 N350 or Core i3-N305 octa-core CPU
summary: The TerraMaster F4-425 Pro NAS features a powerful Intel Core 3 N350 or Core i3-N305 octa-core processor, offering improved performance for storage and networking tasks. It retains the same storage and networking features as its predecessor, including four SATA bays, three M.2 NVMe SSD sockets, and dual 5GbE networking. The device also includes DDR5 memory, USB 3.2 ports, and an HDMI port.
insight: Significant performance boost for demanding NAS applications and tasks.

title: MYiR MAC-B5760 fanless Edge AI industrial PC features Rockchip RK3576 SoC, optional RK1828 LLM/VLM module
summary: MYiR MAC-B5760 is a fanless Edge AI industrial PC with Rockchip RK3576 SoC, offering AI capabilities, networking, and expansion options.
insight: Enables efficient AI processing in industrial settings with compact design.

title: Coin-cell powered Nordic nRF54L15 Tag prototyping platform supports Bluetooth Channel Sounding, Matter, Edge AI
summary: Nordic Semiconductor's nRF54L15 Tag is a coin-cell-powered prototyping platform for ultra-low-power IoT devices, supporting Bluetooth Channel Sounding, Matter, and Edge AI.
insight: Accelerates development of power-efficient IoT devices with advanced wireless capabilities.

title: Optocam Zero A Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W-based DIY digital camera
summary: The Optocam Zero is a DIY digital camera based on Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W, featuring a 1.3-inch LCD, autofocus, and eight photo filters among its key features.
insight: Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W enables accessible and affordable DIY camera projects.

title: Best of Sensors: Why PolyEdge could be the future of Edge AI
summary: Tiami Networks' PolyEdge sensor transforms ambient signals into real-time environmental awareness with a focus on privacy. It integrates multiple functions and sensor modalities to create a comprehensive understanding of its surroundings. This innovation has the potential to revolutionize edge AI applications.
insight: Edge AI meets environmental awareness with private and multifunctional sensing.

title: Many Europeans would avoid companies that rely on US tech firms, survey claims
summary: Four out of five European consumers consider European technology a buying factor. Almost half would avoid companies using US data providers due to privacy concerns.
insight: European consumers prioritize data privacy over US tech company reliance.

title: The Best OLED Gaming Monitors: From $350 QD-OLED to Flagship Tandem WOLEDs
summary: OLED gaming monitors have improved significantly in terms of quality and affordability. Our review covers the top QD-OLED and Tandem WOLED displays across various budgets. We'll help you find the best pick for your gaming needs.
insight: Immersive gaming experiences now accessible to a wider range of gamers.

title: Polymarket paid influencers to fake winning bets, built dummy websites to pull it off
summary: Polymarket paid influencers to fake winning bets, deceiving investors and compromising credibility.
insight: Investor trust undermined by paid influencers faking financial outcomes.

title: Valve confirms FSR 4 for Steam Machine, AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RDNA 3 GPUs
summary: Valve confirms FSR 4 for Steam Machines and AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RDNA 3 GPUs. The update is being tested in the Proton Experimental build for SteamOS. AMD has added FSR 4.1.1 INT8 DLL files to Steam.
insight: Enhanced gaming performance on Steam and AMD GPUs is improved.

title: The founder of Eidos-Montr al says the game industry has been taken over by spreadsheets and lost its soul
summary: Eidos-Montréal's founder believes the game industry has lost its creativity due to corporate influence.
insight: Creative passion squeezed by corporate profit-driven decision-making processes.

title: Valve's Steam Machine is here: starts at $1,049 for 512GB or $1,349 for the 2TB version
summary: Valve's Steam Machine features a semi-custom AMD platform, a 6-core CPU, and 8GB of GDDR6 VRAM. The device is available in two versions: a 512GB model starting at $1,049 and a 2TB model starting at $1,349. It includes a RDNA 3 GPU and 16GB of DDR5 system RAM.
insight: High-performance gaming PC meets affordability and convenience.

title: Anthropic's Mythos AI reportedly cracked NSA classified systems in hours, that would explain the ban
summary: Anthropic's Mythos AI reportedly breached NSA classified systems in a matter of hours, leading to a ban.
insight: Rapid AI breach highlights vulnerabilities in high-security systems and data protection.

title: Microsoft researcher builds a working neural network in Age of Empires II using goats
summary: Adrian de Wynter is an AI scientist at Microsoft and a researcher at the University of York. In addition to studying the fundamental challenges of AI and related technologies, he is also a longtime gamer who has been playing Age of Empires II since 1999. In a recent study, de... Read Entire Article

title: How Pixar recovered Toy Story 2 after a Unix command deleted nearly the entire film in 1998
summary: Pixar's animation pipeline was vulnerable to accidental deletions due to open access. A Unix command intended for cleanup accidentally deleted nearly the entire Toy Story 2 film in 1998. The studio recovered the film through a massive effort.
insight: Open collaboration systems can lead to unintended catastrophic data loss.

title: The FDA just cleared a maggot-based wound treatment from a Singapore startup
summary: The FDA cleared Medifly Maggots, a maggot-based wound treatment, for prescription use. Produced by Singapore-based Cuprina, the product is for maggot debridement therapy. This treatment aims to aid in wound healing.
insight: Innovative maggot therapy offers alternative wound healing solution options

title: Before it was known as Adobe Photoshop, what was the original name of the software in 1987?
summary: Photoshop originated as a student project, developed by Thomas and John Knoll in 1987. The software was initially called 'Display' before Thomas and John renamed it. The Knoll brothers eventually partnered with Adobe to market the software.
insight: Innovation often starts as a humble side project or student endeavor.
